metadata.seed: I hate looking at myself in the mirror, every time I do I feel like the ugliest person in the whole world. I feel like nobody will ever love me.

patient.query: I hate looking at myself in the mirror, every time I do I feel like the ugliest person in the whole world. I feel like nobody will ever love me.

automatic_thoughts: I am the ugliest person in the whole world

neutral: Does not recognize self in mirror (finding)
Own reflection in mirror seems unreal (finding)

Those thoughts just stick with me all day, and even though I know I shouldn’t feel this way, it’s hard to shake them off. It feels like they take up so much space in my mind.

patient.query: Yeah, I mean, I guess it's mostly about my skin. I often feel like it’s just never clear enough, and when I see blemishes, it just ruins my whole day. I know it sounds silly, but it makes me want to hide. Any time someone mentions looks or even just compliments someone else, I kind of feel this knot in my stomach. It just makes everything feel worse.

automatic_thoughts: my skin is never clear enough

patient.query: Yeah, it definitely brings up a lot for me. I’ll be okay one moment, and then I see a picture of myself or catch my reflection in a window, and it just hits me. I feel this rush of shame and frustration. Sometimes, it makes me want to avoid going out or meeting people, like, what’s the point if I feel so awful about how I look? It’s confusing because I know everyone has their struggles, but it feels so personal to me.

triggers: catching my reflection in a window
automatic_thoughts: I feel awful about how I look
what's the point if I feel so awful?

llm_response.response: "It sounds like you're having a tough time with your self-image, and it can be overwhelming when you see yourself. Let's explore what triggers these feelings and if there are any alternative ways of thinking about them."

patient.query: Yeah, it really is tough. It’s like I can’t escape that feeling, especially when something triggers it. I guess I just keep comparing myself to others, and it feels so unfair. I wish I could just see myself differently, but every time I try, it feels like I just end up back at square one. It’s frustrating.

automatic_thoughts: I wish I could just see myself differently
I end up back at square one

patient.query: Yeah, I can remember a time when I saw a friend post a selfie online, and all these people were complimenting her. I couldn’t help but think about how much I wished I looked like that. It just felt so unfair because it seemed like everyone else had it figured out, and I was just stuck feeling like this. I know it’s not really true, but it hits hard in the moment.  I guess I just get overwhelmed with those thoughts.

automatic_thoughts: I wish I looked like that
everyone else has it figured out
I am stuck feeling like this

patient.query: Thanks, I appreciate that. It’s just hard to accept sometimes, you know? I want to be okay with who I am, but those comparisons just keep creeping in. I feel like I’m always battling this inner voice that tells me I’m not good enough. I really want to find a way to quiet that voice, but it feels so loud right now.

behaviors: find a way to quiet that voice
